tables.title: Opioid Emergency: Pulse-Present vs Pulseless
rows: Adjunct once CPR is underway; do not delay CPR

tables.title: Naloxone Quick Facts (2025)
rows: Opioid antagonist that reverses opioid effects
Lay rescuers, trained rescuers, and the public
As needed (e.g., ~every 4 min) if breathing stays inadequate
Can wear off before the opioid — watch for re-narcotization
